title: Photoelectric Effect
description: Shine light on a metal surface and watch what happens. The classical wave model predicts that any frequency should eventually eject electrons — but experiment disagrees. Switch between Classical and Quantum modes to see Einstein's 1905 insight: light arrives as discrete photons, each carrying energy E = hν. Below the threshold frequency, no electrons, ever.
interactive_controls: Wavelength slider (150–750 nm) with color swatch
Metal selector: Cs / Na / Zn / Au (different work functions)
Mode: Classical wave model | Quantum photon model
